claim: The Navier-Stokes periodic skew-symmetry proof packet has a source-bound statement, derivation, deterministic finite-mode reference kernel, run receipt, and Crucible assessment that match the bounded nonlinear-energy-transfer claim while preserving the parent Millennium problem as UNVERIFIABLE.
scope: One deterministic smooth two-dimensional periodic finite-mode divergence-free velocity field under declared assumptions. This does not prove Navier-Stokes existence, uniqueness, smoothness, global regularity, turbulence behavior, physical simulation validity, numerical convergence for arbitrary discretizations, or a new law of physics.

checks.name: problem statement exists
checks.evidence: problem.statement.json binds the Clay parent problem boundary to the bounded skew-symmetry subclaim.

checks.name: derivation exists
checks.evidence: subclaim.skew_symmetry.md derives the smooth-periodic integration-by-parts identity and lists non-proof boundaries.

checks.name: reference kernel runs
checks.evidence: run.receipt.json reports bounded_skew_symmetry_probe MATCH and parent_millennium_problem UNVERIFIABLE.

checks.name: Crucible assessment matches
checks.evidence: crucible-run.json reports 3 MATCH, 0 DRIFT, 0 UNVERIFIABLE for scoped identity/boundary claims.

boundary: This packet teaches a bounded PDE skew-symmetry probe. It does not teach or assert a Navier-Stokes Millennium proof.
